content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN segments (l1,r1),(l2,r2),(lN,rN)(l_1, r_1), (l_2, r_2), (l_N, r_N)(l1​,r1​),(l2​,r2​),(lN​,rN​) and two integers AAA and BBB. Let's define playing a game with two parameters (x,y)(x, y)(x,y), where 0≤x≤N0 \le x \le N0≤x≤N, as the following recursive process:


If x=0x = 0x=0, the game ends immediately.
Otherwise, if y≤lxy \le l_xy≤lx​ or rx≤yr_x \le yrx​≤y, end this game and play a game with (x−1,y)(x-1, y)(x−1,y).
Otherwise (if lx<y<rxl_x \lt y \lt r_xlx​<y<rx​), you must choose one of the following options:

Pay AAA coins, end this game and play a game with (x−1,y)(x-1, y)(x−1,y).
Pay BBB coins, end this game and play two independent games — a game with (x−1,lx)(x-1, l_x)(x−1,lx​) and a game with (x−1,rx)(x-1, r_x)(x−1,rx​).


You should answer QQQ independent queries. In each query, you are given two integers xxx and yyy and you should start by playing a game with parameters (x,y)(x, y)(x,y). Your goal is to reach a state when you should not play any games, since you have successfully ended every game you should have played. Find the minimum number of coins required for reaching this state.
constraints: 1≤li≤ri≤2⋅1051 \le l_i \le r_i \le 2 \cdot 10^51≤li​≤ri​≤2⋅105 for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1035 \cdot 10^35⋅103
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1035 \cdot 10^35⋅103
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains four space-separated integers NNN, QQQ, AAA and BBB.
For each iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), the iii-th of the next NNN lines contains two space-separated integers lil_ili​ and rir_iri​.
QQQ lines follow. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers xxx and yyy describing a query.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer — the minimum number of coins.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: One of the strategies that use the minimum number of coins is:

Pay 333 coins and play a game with (3,4)(3, 4)(3,4).
In the game with (3,4)(3, 4)(3,4), you just play a game with (2,4)(2, 4)(2,4), since 444 is outside the 333-rd segment.
In the game with (2,4)(2, 4)(2,4), pay 444 coins and play games with (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3) and (1,5)(1, 5)(1,5).
Both of these games end without paying any more coins. The only thing left is playing games with (0,3)(0, 3)(0,3) and (0,5)(0, 5)(0,5), which end immediately, also without paying any coins.

The total number of coins used in this strategy is 777.
